scope_note: QA Star-G Tensor Orbit Module Cert [504]. CLAIM: QA mod-9 state space is a G-module for G=Z/24Z x Z/8Z x {1} (arXiv:2605.20440 star-G tensor algebra). (SGT_1) sigma preserves orbit type for all 81 pairs — Cosmos, Satellite, Singularity are invariant G-submodules forming a direct sum; (SGT_2) CRT decomposition of Z/24Z: sigma^8 has period 3 on all 72 Cosmos pairs AND sigma^3 has period 8 on all 72 Cosmos pairs; gcd(3,8)=1 and 3*8=24 certifying Z/24Z ≅ Z/3Z x Z/8Z (Chinese Remainder); witness sigma^8(1,1)=(7,1) != (1,1) and sigma^24(1,1)=(1,1); this is the Kronecker factorization of the Cosmos sub-action (Theorem 2 of arXiv:2605.20440); (SGT_3) sigma^4 has period exactly 2 on all 8 Satellite pairs — the Z/8Z antipodal half-period action; witness sigma^4(3,3)=(6,6) != (3,3) and sigma^8(3,3)=(3,3); (SGT_4) sigma^24 = identity on all 81 pairs — lcm(24,8)=24 is the universal period of the full G-module. Theorem NT: continuous group characters (24th/8th roots of unity) are observer projections; G-module equivariance is certified by integer period arithmetic only. NOT CLAIMED: continuous equivariant learning performance; Lean 4 formalization of QA-specific orbit algebra (only referenced; cert [128] provides the pi(9)=24 Lean infrastructure). Lean 4 extension: arXiv:2605.20440 §4 600-line formalization provides the ambient star-G algebra; cert [128] (pi(9)=24) provides QA-specific Lean 4 proof that |Z/24Z|=24 acting on Cosmos. Primary: Nguyen T.T. et al. (2025) arXiv:2605.20440 doi:10.48550/arXiv.2605.20440; Wildberger N.J. (2005) ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8. Derived 2026-06-23.
